steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence: "Researchers George Starks and Tony Bolden have lent their expertise...to an important new initiative ______ Timeline of African American Music, a digital resource that traces the development of African American music." Left of blank: independent clause. Right of blank: a noun phrase ("Timeline of African American Music") followed by an appositive. Rule: a colon after an independent clause introduces an elaboration, list, or appositive.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — initiative the: "...to an important new initiative the Timeline of African American Music..." No punctuation between the independent clause and the noun phrase — the sentence runs together without a clear signal that what follows names the initiative. ✗
  • Choice B — initiative: the: "...to an important new initiative: the Timeline of African American Music, a digital resource..." The colon correctly follows the independent clause and introduces the name of the initiative as an elaboration. ✓
  • Choice C — initiative; the: "...to an important new initiative; the Timeline of African American Music..." A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but "the Timeline of African American Music, a digital resource..." is a noun phrase, not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — initiative. The: "...to an important new initiative. The Timeline of African American Music..." A period creates a new sentence, but "The Timeline of African American Music, a digital resource that traces..." has no verb — it is a fragment. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
